chunk_summary: Tele-nutrition in India emphasizes actionable service linkage prescriptions for NRC, Anganwadi, and school-age programs to ensure continuity across clinics, community services, and schools.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of tele-nutrition in linking families to NRCs, Anganwadi centers, and school health programs.
Learn how to create actionable service linkage prescriptions for nutrition-related interventions.
Explore the integration of digital tools like Poshan Tracker for beneficiary management and monitoring.
key_facts: Tele-nutrition prescriptions should specify where to go, what to carry, and follow-up schedules.
Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs) are critical for stabilizing children with Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM).
Anganwadi centers provide supplementary nutrition and growth monitoring, often aligned with Village Health and Nutrition Days.
Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) supports structured screening and early intervention for school-age children.
Poshan Tracker facilitates beneficiary management and monitoring for Anganwadi services.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Operational Guidelines on Facility Based Management of Children with Severe Acute Malnutrition emphasize the importance of NRCs for stabilization and follow-up care. (Reference 1)
document_key_topics: Integration of NRCs, Anganwadi, and school programs
